content: Just as high school language learning leverages repeated explicit reflection (the original linguistic 'reflexion' practice) to turn new vocabulary into implicit, automatic knowledge, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by integrating spaced, explicit linguistic reflexion rehearsal of correct problem-solving steps, rather than only contrasting inconsistent outputs. This draws on the core educational principle of structured reflexion that has long worked for human learning to fix a key limitation of modern LLM reflection that often only focuses on correcting errors rather than reinforcing robust correct reasoning.
